title: Kugti Sanctuary
tagline: Kugti Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Himachal Pradesh

overview.short: Kugti Sanctuary is the second largest sanctuary in the state of Himachal Pradesh.
overview.description: Kugti Sanctuary is the second largest sanctuary in the state of Himachal Pradesh. Its situated at an altitude ranging from 2,195 m to 5,040 m. On the west side, Tundah Sanctuary surrounds this sanctuary by a forest corridor. The landscape of the sanctuary varies, and it is blessed with an abundance of flora and fauna.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Yangrang
topPlaces.description: Yangrang is a village in the Lahaul and Spiti district of Himachal Pradesh, India. It is 25 km away from Keylong.

items.note: Yangrang is a village in the Lahaul and Spiti district of Himachal Pradesh, India. It is 25 km away from Keylong.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kugti Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kugti Sanctuary is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kugti Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kangra Airport (Gaggal), Dharamshala (76 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (186 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kugti Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kugti Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kugti Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Kugti Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.
